<parameter=code>
# Examine the current state of the left panel objects
curr_nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Focus on left panel objects (col < 32)
left_objects = [n for n in curr_nodes if n['boundary'][0][1] < 32 and n['pixels'] > 1]
print("Left panel objects (multi-pixel):")
for n in sorted(left_objects, key=lambda x: (x['boundary'][0][0], x['boundary'][0][1])):
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check for green block
green_nodes = [n for n in curr_nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"\nGreen (N) nodes: {[(n['id'], n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in green_nodes]}")

# Check blue cursor
blue_nodes = [n for n in curr_nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] <= 4]
print(f"\nSmall blue nodes (cursor?): {[(n['id'], n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in blue_nodes]}")

# Check right panel objects
right_objects = [n for n in curr_nodes if n['boundary'][0][1] >= 32 and n['pixels'] > 1]
print(f"\nRight panel objects: {[(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in sorted(right_objects, key=lambda x: x['boundary'][0][0])]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
